description:
Add optional `model?: string` to each of the 5 MCP tool input schemas (review_plan, review_code, review_precommit, review_status, review_history — though status/history do not start threads, so probably only the 3 reviewing tools). Callers pass a model per request; the server forwards it to startThread({ model: input.model ?? config.model }). Config value becomes the default, not the only option.

## Why this matters

When OpenAI announces a new flagship model, it lands in the API tier immediately but lags on the ChatGPT-subscription Codex tier by a few days. During that window, a gpt-5.5 default rejects every call with "not supported when using Codex with a ChatGPT account." v0.3.2 shipped a targeted error tip that instructs Claude Code to fall back to gpt-5.4 — but today Claude Code cannot actually act on that advice without: (a) editing .reviewbridge.json, (b) restarting the MCP server so the loader re-runs, and (c) retrying. Per-call override lets Claude Code obey the tip immediately in the next tool call, no restart, no config edit, no silent behavior.

## Design

- Schema: add `model?: string` to review_plan, review_code, review_precommit input schemas (Zod).
- Client: thread each method signature to accept an optional override and pass it to `startThread({ model })`.
- Resolution order: explicit tool-call `model` → `.reviewbridge.json` `model` → built-in default.
- Session-ID interaction: if `session_id` is passed AND `model` is passed, reject with a clear error ("cannot change model mid-session; start a new session if you need a different model"). A thread is bound to its original model at creation; swapping mid-session is not a real operation.
- Multi-chunk reviews: chunks 2..N resume chunk 1's thread via session_id, so they inherit chunk 1's resolved model automatically — no extra plumbing needed.

## Why this over the alternatives

Considered and rejected earlier today:
- Silent fallback chain (config-driven retry on MODEL_ERROR) — silent downgrade is a trust smell; users expect the model they asked for.
- Revert default to gpt-5.4 — throws away the "latest by default" pitch for a ~1 week rollout gap and forces a second release when rollout completes.
- Probe before each review — extra RTT on the happy path.

Per-call override dominates: no silent behavior, no default churn, no extra latency, and it composes with the already-shipped targeted error tip (tip tells caller what to do; override lets them do it).

## Acceptance

- Passing `model` in a tool call uses that model for the thread.
- Omitting `model` falls back to config, then to built-in default.
- Passing both `model` and `session_id` returns a structured error.
- Multi-chunk path: chunk 1 sees the override; chunks 2..N resume chunk 1's thread and inherit.
- README gains a short row documenting the field under each tool.
- Tests: per-tool override happy path, session_id + model rejection, multi-chunk inheritance.

## Scope estimate

~10 LOC in src/codex/client.ts (thread signatures + resolution helper), 3 × schema additions in src/tools/review-*.ts, 1 README row per tool, 3-5 tests. Ships as v0.3.3. No breaking changes; config and built-in default paths are unchanged for existing users.

## Source

Surfaced in an interactive test of v0.3.2 on 2026-04-23: Claude Code received the fallback tip from the classifier but could not act on it without MCP restart. Filed as follow-up to the v0.3.0 → v0.3.2 model-rollout work.
title: Per-call model override in MCP tool input schemas
